title: Proxy selector `0x03b79c24` can transfer custodied `weETH` to an attacker-controlled recipient
claim: The forked PoC shows that a direct call to the live ERC1967 proxy with selector `0x03b79c24` and an attacker-chosen recipient succeeds, after which the attacker contract has enough `weETH` to pay the Uniswap V3 swap callback and unwrap `114.534059890882021484` WETH to ETH. This is strong evidence that the proxied implementation exposed an externally callable path that released the proxy's `weETH` to an arbitrary receiver without effective access control.
impact: Any attacker can drain protocol-held `weETH` from the proxy and convert it into liquid assets, causing direct theft of TVL.
paths: Call the proxy directly with selector `0x03b79c24` and an attacker-controlled recipient
Receive transferable `weETH` on the attacker contract
Swap the stolen position through the Uniswap V3 pool and pay the callback with the drained `weETH`
Unwrap the acquired WETH and forward the ETH profit to the attacker
